q: The air flow moving above the top curved surface of an aeroplane wing has a shorter distance to travel than the lower flat surface.
High velocity of air flow along the top surface of a wing causes a low pressure above the wing.
The pressure difference between the lower and upper surfaces of an aeroplane wing provides a lift on the wing.
When the piston of an insect sprayer is pressed, the liquid inside the container rushes up because it is at a lower pressure than the nozzle.
The Magnus effect is named after the German physicist Heinrich Gustav Magnus, who described the effect in 1852.
During cyclones, the reduced pressure inside the house causes the roof to fly away.
